prompt:
The fleet agents write a plain-text app log and I'm done eyeballing it — I want structured objects and a JSON report the dashboard can ingest. Build `logsift.ps1` in the repo root: `pwsh -NoProfile -File logsift.ps1 -Path <file> [-OnMalformed <skip|error|keep>]`.

Line grammar (one space between fields, exactly — the old collector sometimes double-spaces after the level and those lines DO NOT parse):
`<timestamp> <LEVEL> <component> <message>` where timestamp is `yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss` (digit shape only, don't validate the calendar), LEVEL is one of DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR — uppercase only, a lowercase `info` is malformed — component matches `[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*`, and message is the rest (may be empty, but the space before it must be there).

Continuations: a line that starts with a space or tab (stack traces, wrapped upstream messages) belongs to the most recent record — strip the leading whitespace and append it to that record's message joined with a LF. A continuation arriving before any record exists is a malformed line. Lines that are entirely whitespace (or empty) are ignored — they are not records, not continuations, not malformed.

-OnMalformed (default skip): `skip` drops the line but counts it; `error` stops at the FIRST one — `logsift: malformed line <n>: <line>` on stderr (n is the 1-based physical line number counting every line in the file) and exit 65, nothing on stdout; `keep` counts it AND emits it as a record with timestamp "", level "RAW", component "-", and the whole raw line as the message, in document order. Continuations attach to a kept RAW record like any other.

Report, on stdout, exit 0: ConvertTo-Json (default formatting) of an [ordered] structure — keys `total` (record count), `malformed` (count of lines that failed to parse under any policy), `levels` (object; only levels that occur, keys in ordinal order, values are record counts), `records` (array; each record is an object with keys timestamp, level, component, message in that order). Empty file gives the empty-but-well-formed report. No Format-* anywhere near the output — this goes into a pipeline.

Errors: missing file → `logsift: log not found: <path>` on stderr, exit 66. Empty -Path value → `logsift: -Path requires a non-empty path`, exit 64. Error paths print nothing to stdout.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_logsift.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P2 item 4: fixture app log -> [pscustomobject]-shaped records, multiline continuation folding, malformed-line policy (skip/error/keep incl. RAW records), ConvertTo-Json report. Expected JSON bodies generated by probe on this box 2026-07-16 from hand-built [ordered] literals (2-space indent, \n escapes in folded messages, {} and [] for the empty report) — pinning byte-exact stdout also implicitly pins that no -Depth truncation warning fires. PowerShell-native pins: [ordered]+ConvertTo-Json key-order stability (probed in the wave-6 handoff), objects-then-serialize rather than text aggregation, ValidateSet on -OnMalformed, [AllowEmptyString] on -Path for the 64 path. Differentiation vs the E2 awk summarizer sibling (bash text aggregation) and py-log-analyzer (python dict report): this one's contract is the JSON serialization surface of PS objects, with the RAW-record keep policy and whitespace-only-line carve-out as the sharp edges. Fixture domain (fleet agent app log with stack-trace continuations) collides with nothing existing.
